Not exactly what I would refer to as "enlightening." This museum privides a very brief and basic overview of the history of Seattle. In fact, it's so basic that it's hardly engaging at all.
The museum of history and industry gives a great insight into Seattle background in maritime and aviation. The restored ships on Union Lake that are part of the museum are amazing.

This museum is inviting and shares a wide variety of information about the history of Seattle and examples of innovation of Seattle businesses. There was a Exhibit about Nelson Mandela that was excellent portrayal of his life and impact on society.
